summary: Plugin Installer feature fully tested. Critical backend bug found and fixed: _require_role was not defined in install_plugin and uninstall_plugin endpoints, causing 500 errors. Fixed to use require_admin(). All 18 backend tests pass (18/18). Full E2E UI flow working: sidebar menu item visible, ComponentInstaller loads, empty state shows, JSON paste enables Preview btn, Preview loads correctly, Install works, plugin appears in list, Uninstall/Rollback works, empty state restored.

critical.issue: NameError: _require_role is not defined - install endpoint returned 500 for all requests. FIXED: replaced _require_role(current_user, [...]) with require_admin(current_user) in server.py line 17561
Same NameError: _require_role not defined in uninstall_plugin. FIXED: replaced with require_admin(current_user) at server.py line 17626

issues: Plugin Installer menu item is near bottom of sidebar - requires scrolling to see it. This is expected per design spec (between Database and API Tools).

action_items: FIXED: _require_role replaced with require_admin in both install_plugin and uninstall_plugin endpoints - already fixed in this iteration

NOTE: ComponentInstaller.js uses useState(() => { loadPlugins(); }) instead of useEffect for initial data load (line 312). This works because the useState initializer runs once, but it's non-standard React pattern and should be refactored to useEffect(). Not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: CRITICAL BUG FIXED: _require_role called in install_plugin (line 17561) and uninstall_plugin (line 17626) is undefined. Correct function is require_admin(). Both now fixed.
Minor: ComponentInstaller.js line 312 uses useState(() => { loadPlugins(); }) as an effect trigger instead of useEffect. Works but is non-idiomatic React and could cause issues in strict mode.
Note: route order in server.py - /plugins/preview and /plugins/install must come before /plugins/{plugin_id} to avoid path conflicts. Currently correct.
seed_data_creation: TEST_plugin_installer_v1, TEST_e2e_lifecycle_plugin created and cleaned up. test-plugin installed and uninstalled via UI.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Plugin Installer backend+frontend fully verified. All 4 routes working: GET /api/plugins, POST /api/plugins/preview, POST /api/plugins/install, DELETE /api/plugins/{id}. Key bug fixed in this iteration: _require_role -> require_admin. Frontend route is /dev-settings (not /admin/dev-settings). Login requires using data-testid selectors (login-email-input, login-password-input, login-submit-btn) and page.type() not page.fill() for React controlled inputs.
verified_features: ✅ Plugin Installer menu item in sidebar (data-testid='dev-menu-plugins') - between Database and API Tools
✅ Click Plugin Installer → ComponentInstaller UI loads (data-testid='component-installer')
✅ Empty state: 'No plugins installed yet' visible when no plugins installed
✅ POST /api/plugins/preview with valid JSON bundle returns preview data (200 OK, valid=true)
✅ POST /api/plugins/install installs plugin and returns success status (200 OK)
✅ Installed plugin appears in GET /api/plugins list
✅ Preview button (data-testid='preview-plugin-btn') disabled initially, enabled after JSON pasted
✅ Install button (data-testid='install-plugin-btn') disabled initially, enabled after preview loaded
✅ Full E2E: paste JSON → preview → install → plugin in list → uninstall → empty state
rca_of_issues: ROOT CAUSE: install_plugin and uninstall_plugin endpoints called _require_role(current_user, ['super_admin', 'admin']) which is undefined in server.py. The correct function is require_admin(current_user) defined at line 1241. This caused all POST /api/plugins/install and DELETE /api/plugins/{id} calls to return 500 Internal Server Error. Fix: replace _require_role with require_admin in both endpoints.
